definition: Context-sensitive coverage splits the edge-map reward for a sparse, exact-value gate across many call-context slots, diluting the feedback signal that should steer mutation toward the required literal byte or codepoint; the corpus fills with context-duplicate seeds that never carry the needed value.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

signature.mechanism_summary: Context-sensitive coverage dilutes the edge-map signal that would reinforce the xMl name byte across call contexts, so corpus fills with context-duplicate seeds that never carry the m/M byte at name[1].
signature.one_line: ctx coverage dilutes xMl name-byte signal across call-context splits

analysis.summary_one_line: A parsed PI-target name whose first three chars are x/X,m/M,l/L (e.g. literal xMl inside <?xMl) to take name[1]=='m'/'M' true

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An XML processing instruction whose target name parses to a 3+ char token beginning x/X then m/M then l/L, so that name[1] is 'm' or 'M'; the winner needs the literal byte run '<?xMl' (or '<?xml') where the second name char is m/M, not an arbitrary perturbation like '<?x_l'.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The naive winner-resolving seed carries the literal bytes '<?xMl version=' at offsets 0x16-0x1d ('3c(<) 3f(?) 78(x) 4d(M) 6c(l) 20( )'), so xmlParsePITarget sees name[0]=='x',name[1]=='M',name[2]=='l' and the line 5129 true side is taken; W shows the blocker true side hit (T=3 F=0).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive_ctx loser seeds carry corrupted PI targets ('<?x_l' with byte 5f('_') at the name[1] position, plus xmltp/xsim variants), so name[1] is not m/M and line 5129 only ever takes the false side; the per-role overlay records the blocker exclusively as L false (T=0 F=12).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive (the winner) uses a bare per-edge MaxMapFeedback, so once havoc/token mutation lands an 'x?l'->'xMl' byte that flips line 5128 to true the new edge at 5129 is immediately rewarded. naive_ctx (the loser) folds a call-context hash into the edge-map index (CtxHook), so the same parser edges are split across many (call-context, edge) slots; the feedback signal that should guide mutation toward the xMl name is diluted across contexts, the corpus fills with context-duplicate inputs that never carry a valid xml-prefixed PI target, and the branch stays blocked in all 10 trials.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the name[1]=='m'/'M' check at parser.c:5129, true side globally blocked.
The decisive pair is naive beating naive_ctx with delta ctx_coverage; naive_ctx is the loser blocked in all 10 trials.
The winner resolved 8/10 while naive_ctx resolved 0/10 and stayed blocked 10/10.
The winner-resolving seed contains the literal '<?xMl version=' byte run carrying x,M,l as the name characters.
A loser-blocking seed has a corrupted PI target '<?x_l' where the name[1] position is '_' (0x5f) rather than m/M.
On the winner the blocker true side is taken (T=3 F=0) while on the loser it is only ever false (T=0 F=12).
naive's feedback is a bare per-edge hit-count bucket with no context indexing.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: per-edge hit-count bucket; a new bucket triggers a

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx folds a call-context hash into the edge-map index so the same edge is recorded at different map slots per call path.

folds it into the edge-map index, so the same basic-block edge is
recorded at different map slots depending on the call path that
reached it.

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx treats a new (call-context, edge) pair as new coverage rather than a bare edge, which fragments the signal.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
a "new bucket" is a new
(call-context, edge) pair rather than a bare edge.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with the same x/m/l three-char PI-target gate where naive_ctx resolves the branch as fast as or faster than naive across trials, showing the context-indexed edge map does not dilute the feedback that guides mutation toward the literal name bytes.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The attribution that context-hash fragmentation (not mere run-to-run variance over a single 3-byte gate) is what blocks naive_ctx in 10/10 trials rests on mechanism reasoning rather than direct evidence of corpus context-duplication.

signature.mechanism_summary: Context-sensitive coverage folds call-context into edge-map indices; for this shallow fixed call chain it adds no useful signal but inflates the map, dispersing mutation effort across sibling switch arms instead of converging on the exact codepoints.
signature.one_line: context coverage inflates map for shallow chain, disperses effort from exact codepoint

analysis.summary_one_line: Buffer must carry Tirhuta codepoint 0x1148B/0x1148D (LE 8b/8d 14 01 00) at cur to reach this switch case; baseline havoc finds it, technique-enabled variants get diverted to sibling codepoints.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Same feature: the current glyph in a TIRHUTA buffer must encode codepoint 0x1148B or 0x1148D (LE 8b/8d 14 01 00) so the switch reaches case 0x1148Bu/0x1148Du at line 409.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive (winner) also stores the 8d 14 01 00 run (winner-resolving Seed 5, fuzzer=naive, contains 8d 8d 8d 14 01 00 near offset 0x0a), reaching the blocker case via plain havoc edge-guided mutation.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive_ctx (loser) blocked seeds carry sibling/neighboring 4-byte values like ba 14 01 00 and b0 14 01 00 (e.g. loser-blocking Seed 1 fuzzer=naive_ctx) which route to other Tirhuta cases (0x114B0 etc.), so the exact 0x1148B/0x1148D codepoint is absent and line 409 true is never reached (L hits T=0).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: The winner is the baseline naive fuzzer using havoc + edge feedback. naive_ctx adds context-sensitive coverage (the CtxHook folds the call context into the edge-map index). Here that is counter-productive: the call chain into _hb_preprocess_text_vowel_constraints is shallow and fixed (1-hop from preprocess_text_use), so context-indexing yields no new useful coverage signal but inflates the map, spending the loser's effort exploring sibling switch arms instead of converging on the specific 0x1148B/0x1148D codepoint. The winner's enabling technique is plain havoc, not context coverage.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked branch is the TIRHUTA switch case 0x1148Bu/0x1148Du, true side globally blocked.
Both decisive pairs have the simpler baseline fuzzer as winner and the technique-enabled variant as loser.
Pair 2 is the naive > naive_ctx ctx_coverage delta.
A value_profile winner seed carries the literal codepoint bytes 8d 14 01 00 at offset 0.
A naive winner seed also carries the 8d ... 14 01 00 codepoint run.
The winner takes the line 409 true side (T=14) while the loser does not (T=0).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 409  T=14 F=91  T=0 F=195  case 0x1148Bu: case 0x1148Du:  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: A value_profile_cmplog loser seed carries 81 14 01 00, the sibling 0x11481 codepoint, not the blocker case bytes.
A naive_ctx loser seed carries ba 14 01 00 (0x114BA) rather than the 8b/8d 14 01 00 case codepoint.
The TIRHUTA case 0x1148Bu/0x1148Du requires the following glyph to equal 0x114BA for matched, confirming the switch dispatches on cur().codepoint.
value_profile uses only havoc + token mutators with no I2SRandReplace.
value_profile_cmplog adds the I2SRandReplace mutator on top of havoc.
naive_ctx folds call context into the edge-map index, recording the same edge at different slots per call path.

folds it into the edge-map index, so the same basic-block edge is

evidence_trail.claim: The call chain into the enclosing function is a shallow fixed 1-hop from preprocess_text_use.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with a single switch case gated on an exact 4-byte little-endian codepoint, swept against value_profile vs value_profile_cmplog (and naive vs naive_ctx), in which the technique-enabled variant reaches the case AS OFTEN OR MORE than the baseline — i.e. no anti-divergence — would refute the claim that the literal-codepoint feature is found by havoc and diluted by I2S/ctx-coverage.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The mechanism for WHY the technique-enabled losers underperform (I2S latching onto sibling Tirhuta operands; ctx-coverage inflating the map without useful signal) is inferred from seed byte patterns and mechanism descriptions, not directly observed in a controlled per-operand trace.
